title: santalum insulare var. hendersonense
intro: Santalum insulare var. hendersonense, also known as Henderson's Sandalwood, is a rare and endangered variant of the Santalum insulare species, native to the island of Henderson in the Pitcairn Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to various health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and reduced fever. The oil extracted from Henderson's Sandalwood is used in herbal preparations, including essential oils, perfumes, and aromatherapy products, due to its calming and relaxing effects. Additionally, the plant is valued for its potential to aid in stress relief, improve sleep quality, and promote overall well-being.
